product.one_line_description: The Threads API enables third-party platforms to publish posts, retrieve posts and replies, and manage basic insights on Threads accounts.

authentication.auth_summary: The Threads API uses the OAuth 2.0 protocol for authentication and authorization. Each API request must be authenticated with an access token, which can be obtained after a user grants the app permissions through an OAuth dialog. For long-lived access, the Instagram Graph API can generate User Access Tokens with up to 60-day expiry.

developer_access.access_notes: To use the Threads API, developers must have a Facebook Developer Account and an app that has successfully passed App Review for Threads API access. The App Review process involves human approval to grant the necessary permissions for API usage.

api_surface.api_summary: The Threads API provides functionality to publish posts, retrieve an app's own posts, retrieve replies to an app's posts, and manage basic insights. It also supports responding to specific posts, hiding or unhiding replies, deleting media posts, and reading media posts including comments, likes, replies, and user profile information like follower count. The API operates via HTTP requests and offers webhook capabilities.

mcp.mcp_summary: No mention of Model Context Protocol (MCP) or similar AI agent integration protocols was found in the provided documentation for the Threads API.

buildability.primary_blocker: Requires vendor/support approval for admin credentials.
buildability.verdict_reasoning: [PROVISIONAL V1] Admin gated access explicitly requires external vendor authorization.

evidence_pool.claim: The Threads API enables third-party platforms to publish posts to Threads, retrieve their own posts, retrieve replies to their own posts, and manage basic insights.
evidence_pool.source_title: Threads API - Documentation - Meta for Developers
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: The Threads API enables third-party platforms to publish posts to Threads, retrieve their own posts, retrieve replies to their own posts, and manage basic insights.

evidence_pool.source_title: Threads API - Documentation - Meta for Developers
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: The Threads API uses the OAuth 2.0 protocol for authentication and authorization.

evidence_pool.claim: The Threads API uses the OAuth 2.0 protocol for authentication and authorization, requiring access tokens for each API request. Long-lived access tokens can be obtained via the Instagram Graph API.
evidence_pool.source_title: Threads API - Documentation - Meta for Developers
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
The Threads API uses the OAuth 2.0 protocol for authentication and authorization. Each API request must be authenticated with an access token.
For applications that require long-lived access, we recommend using the Threads API with the Instagram Graph API. The Instagram Graph API uses OAuth 2.0 to generate User Access Tokens with up to 60-day expiry.

evidence_pool.source_title: Threads API - Documentation - Meta for Developers
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: To get access to the Threads API, your app must first pass App Review.

evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: To get access to the Threads API, your app must first pass App Review.

evidence_pool.claim: Developers must have a Facebook Developer Account and an app that has passed App Review for Threads API access. The App Review process requires human approval to grant the necessary permissions for API usage.
evidence_pool.source_title: Threads API - Documentation - Meta for Developers
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
Before you can use the Threads API, you must have:
A Facebook Developer Account.
An app that has passed App Review for Threads API access.

evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
Webhooks
Subscribe to Instagram API Webhooks to receive notifications when changes happen on an Instagram user's Threads profile or media.

evidence_pool.source_title: Threads API - Documentation - Meta for Developers
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
What you can do
Publish posts to Threads
Retrieve your own posts
Retrieve replies to your own posts
Manage basic insights
Respond to specific posts
Hide or unhide replies
Delete media posts
Read media posts, including comments, likes, replies, and other metadata
Read user profiles, including follower count and other metadata

evidence_pool.claim: The Threads API provides endpoints for publishing posts, retrieving posts and their replies, managing basic insights, responding to specific posts, hiding/unhiding replies, deleting media posts, and reading user profiles and media post metadata. It supports standard HTTP requests and offers webhook functionality.
evidence_pool.source_title: Threads API - Documentation - Meta for Developers
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
What you can do
Publish posts to Threads
Retrieve your own posts
Retrieve replies to your own posts
Manage basic insights
Respond to specific posts
Hide or unhide replies
Delete media posts
Read media posts, including comments, likes, replies, and other metadata
Read user profiles, including follower count and other metadata
All API requests must be authenticated with an access token. See our Authentication documentation for more details. HTTP requests
Webhooks
Subscribe to Instagram API Webhooks to receive notifications when changes happen on an Instagram user's Threads profile or media.
